skill.name: inplasy-registration-helper
skill.description: Assists researchers in generating INPLASY registration content for meta-analyses from a title and optional protocol. Use when the user wants to draft a INPLASY registration form.
skill.summary: Generates structured INPLASY protocol registration drafts for systematic reviews and meta-analyses from a study title and optional protocol document.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 41 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched JSON metadata fields and Markdown text with no actual cryptographic operations. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are standard Markdown code formatting backticks in documentation files, not Ruby execution. The skill runs a single benign Python script (date_utils.py) using only stdlib datetime for date calculation. No network exfiltration, code obfuscation, prompt injection, or system reconnaissance detected. The GitHub URL reference is legitimate source attribution.

content.user_title: Generate INPLASY registration forms for systematic reviews
content.value_statement: Researchers waste time manually formatting systematic review registration content. This skill generates complete INPLASY registration drafts from just a study title and optional protocol document.
actual_capabilities: Generates complete INPLASY registration form drafts from a study title and optional protocol
Extracts PICO elements to inform search strategy and eligibility criteria
Calculates study timeline with start date and 28-day anticipated completion date
Produces 16 standardized INPLASY form sections following official templates
Preserves system dropdown placeholders (curly braces) while filling analysis placeholders (angle brackets)
limitations: Cannot submit forms to the INPLASY website (manual submission required)
Does not verify the scientific accuracy or validity of generated content
Requires at minimum a study title to produce a complete draft
The 28-day completion timeline is a fixed default value

use_cases.title: First-time systematic review registration
use_cases.description: A graduate student starting their first meta-analysis provides a study title and receives a complete INPLASY registration draft without needing to study the form structure.

use_cases.title: Protocol-to-registration conversion
use_cases.description: A clinical research team with an existing protocol document transforms it into the structured INPLASY registration format for official submission.

use_cases.title: Rapid update for existing reviews
use_cases.description: A researcher updating a systematic review generates an updated INPLASY registration with new timeline and revised criteria without starting from scratch.

prompt_templates.title: Basic registration from title
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an INPLASY registration form for a systematic review with the title: [Enter your study title]. Use the standard INPLASY template sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has only a study title and needs a complete draft

prompt_templates.title: Registration from title and protocol
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the attached protocol document and study title '[Enter title]', generate a complete INPLASY registration form. Fill in all template sections with details from the protocol.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an existing protocol and wants to derive registration content

prompt_templates.title: Registration with PICO extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract PICO elements from '[Enter title]' and generate a complete INPLASY registration. Population: [specify]. Intervention: [specify]. Comparison: [specify]. Outcome: [specify]. Produce all 16 sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to define PICO elements for precise registration

prompt_templates.title: Scripted workflow with timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Run scripts/date_utils.py to get the timeline. Then generate a complete INPLASY registration for '[Enter title]' using references/registration_templates.md. Preserve curly brace placeholders. Fill all angle bracket placeholders. Output all 16 sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a fully automated workflow with date calculation and complete form generation

output_examples.input: Generate an INPLASY registration for 'Effectiveness of cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia in older adults'
output_examples.output:
1. Title: The effectiveness of cognitive behavioral therapy compared with usual care for insomnia in older adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis protocol.
2. Review question: To evaluate the effects of CBT-I compared with usual care on sleep quality in patients aged 65 and older, based on RCTs.
3. Eligibility criteria: RCTs with older adults diagnosed with insomnia. Intervention: CBT-I. Comparator: usual care or sleep hygiene education. Primary outcomes: PSQI score, sleep efficiency.

output_examples.input: What dates should I use for my INPLASY registration?
output_examples.output:
Start Date: 28/05/2026
Anticipated Completion Date: 25/06/2026
These dates use a standard 28-day completion period. The start date is set to today.

output_examples.input: Generate a complete INPLASY registration from my protocol for 'Exercise therapy for knee osteoarthritis'
output: INPLASY registration generated with all 16 sections filled.
Information sources: PubMed, Embase, Cochrane Library, Web of Science, PEDro.
Risk of bias: Cochrane Risk of Bias tool for RCTs.
Data synthesis: Meta-analysis using RevMan. Risk ratios for dichotomous outcomes, mean differences for WOMAC scores.
Subgroup analysis: By exercise type (strengthening vs aerobic vs combined), by disease severity.
All curly brace placeholders preserved. All angle bracket placeholders filled.

best_practices: Always provide a specific study title with population, intervention, comparison, and outcome for best results
Review all generated content for scientific accuracy and completeness before submitting to INPLASY
Attach a protocol document when available to improve accuracy of eligibility criteria and search strategy
anti_patterns: Do not use for non-systematic reviews or narrative reviews that do not require protocol registration
Do not submit generated content without verifying the accuracy of PICO elements and search strategy
Do not skip providing a protocol document when one is available, as this reduces output quality

faq.question: What is INPLASY?
faq.answer: INPLASY (International Platform of Registered Systematic Review and Meta-analysis Protocols) is a public registry for systematic review protocols. This skill helps you draft the registration content.

faq.question: Do I need to install any software?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses a Python script from the standard library for date calculations and text templates for form sections. Your AI assistant handles both.

faq.question: Can this skill submit my registration to INPLASY?
faq.answer: No. The skill only generates the registration text content. You must submit it manually through the INPLASY website.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide?
faq.answer: At minimum, a study title. For better results, also attach a protocol document with your research question, methods, and analysis plan.

faq.question: Does this work with PROSPERO?
faq.answer: The templates are designed for INPLASY format. PROSPERO has different registration requirements. Use a PROSPERO-specific skill if needed.

faq.question: How long does the registration process take?
faq.answer: The skill generates content immediately. The timeline uses a 28-day completion default, but actual INPLASY review and approval times vary.
